description.ko: 보이지 않는 소리가 고요한 티타늄 표면 위의 가는 빛의 파형을 끌어당기는 미니멀한 ECHO-0 공명 연구입니다.
description.en: A minimal ECHO-0 resonance study where invisible sound pulls bright hairline waves across a silent titanium surface.

prompt: ECHO-0 sound visualization rendered as a silent macro study of a brushed titanium resonance plate, viewed from a low grazing angle with wide clean negative space and no visible sound source. Across the satin metal surface, hair-thin #4169E1 royal-blue waveform filaments skim just above shallow engraved grooves, their paths vibrating into rapid micro-jitters as if invisible frequencies are tugging them sideways. Sudden percussive transients appear as clipped #FF4500 orange impact notches and tiny angular displacements along the blue traces, while sparse #FFD700 gold consonance dots mark only a few exact phase alignments. Rare #32CD32 lime-green timing ticks stand upright like small calibration marks at abrupt pitch changes. The composition stays minimal and rhythmic: low particle count, crisp line shadows, fast staccato motion, precise amplitude bends, bright palette against cool metal, resonance in silence made visible as vibrating frequencies reacting to unheard sound.
